title: The Car Pool Lane

jokes.text: Yeah. How do you work a glue gun? Oh, yeah, like I know how to work a glue gun.
jokes.setup: Jeff asks a practical question about using a glue gun
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic response reveals he's equally clueless about basic tools
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry self-awareness about his incompetence, delivered with perfect sarcasm

jokes.text: Get the violin out. That's pretty sad, yeah.
jokes.setup: Larry suggests getting out a violin in response to their pathetic situation
jokes.punchline: Jeff's deadpan agreement that it's 'pretty sad' emphasizes how low they've sunk
jokes.explanation: Understated commentary on their pitiful state, enhanced by Jeff's matter-of-fact agreement

jokes.text: It is weird to ask a guy, you know his father just died, can we use your tickets? It is kind of tacky to do that.
jokes.setup: Jeff acknowledges how inappropriate it would be to ask for the tickets
jokes.punchline: His awareness of the tackiness while clearly planning to do it anyway
jokes.explanation: Jeff's self-aware hypocrisy - knowing something is wrong while planning to do it

jokes.text: Marty, I know you're still in mourning, but is there any... I feel your grief, but are you gonna use those tickets?
jokes.setup: Larry rehearses how he might approach Marty about the tickets
jokes.punchline: The grotesquely insensitive phrasing that juxtaposes grief with ticket requests
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy - Larry's complete inability to navigate social sensitivity

jokes.text: If the subject of baseball comes up, it comes up, that's all.
jokes.setup: Larry continues justifying their 'coincidental' visit
jokes.punchline: The obvious pre-planning disguised as spontaneity
jokes.explanation: Larry's continued self-deception about obviously orchestrated encounter

jokes.text: Little turkey action today. Turkey today.
jokes.setup: Marty recommends the turkey at the restaurant
jokes.punchline: His oddly enthusiastic and repetitive phrasing about turkey
jokes.explanation: Mildly funny character quirk in phrasing, but not particularly strong

jokes.text: Great course, have you played it? Uh no, I'm a Jew.
jokes.setup: Larry asks if Marty has played Beverly Park golf course
jokes.punchline: Marty's matter-of-fact acknowledgment of country club antisemitism
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and dark humor about antisemitism in country clubs. Delivered with perfect deadpan timing.

jokes.text: Hey, if you're a little lonely, you need a little companionship at the game, if you want me to keep you company, I'd be happy to do it.
jokes.setup: Marty says he's still going to the game despite his father's death
jokes.punchline: Larry's transparent attempt to get tickets disguised as compassionate offer
jokes.explanation: Larry's shameless manipulation disguised as compassion - classic character behavior

jokes.text: What about your dad's seat? It's spoken for.
jokes.setup: Larry suggests using the dead father's seat
jokes.punchline: Marty's matter-of-fact rejection - the dead man's seat is 'spoken for'
jokes.explanation: Absurdity of a dead person's seat being 'spoken for' while Larry is right there

jokes.text: The tip, remember the weatherman's tip? / The statute of limitations has gotta be up on that, no?
jokes.setup: Reference to previous golf tip situation
jokes.punchline: Larry applying legal concept to golf advice grudge
jokes.explanation: Clever callback showing Larry's legalistic thinking applied to petty grievances

jokes.text: I'm trying to get out of jury duty today. They hate people in the entertainment industry.
jokes.setup: Larry explains why he's wearing the Seinfeld jacket
jokes.punchline: His cynical manipulation of anti-Hollywood bias to avoid civic duty
jokes.explanation: Larry's typical scheme to avoid responsibility through manipulation

jokes.text: I would serve if they made me the foreman, but... I can't serve under another foreman.
jokes.setup: Larry discusses jury service
jokes.punchline: His absurd ego requiring him to be in charge of jury duty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David narcissism - even civic duty must revolve around his ego

jokes.text: That's pretty high up in the alphabet. I'm just joking around.
jokes.setup: Cheryl comments on good seats (section letters)
jokes.punchline: Larry's dad joke about alphabet positioning, then immediate acknowledgment it's bad
jokes.explanation: Weak dad joke that Larry himself acknowledges is bad

jokes.text: Do I look like some kind of beatnik? I'm not gonna... with the marijuana.
jokes.setup: Larry suggests marijuana for Nat's glaucoma
jokes.punchline: Nat's outdated 'beatnik' reference revealing his age and square nature
jokes.explanation: Nat's generational disconnect shown through outdated slang

jokes.text: My cousin once stole an Almond Joy from me. It was upsetting at the time, but, um...
jokes.setup: Lawyer asks Larry about being victim of serious crime
jokes.punchline: Larry's ridiculous example of candy theft as his worst victimization
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist response that reveals Larry's privileged life experience

jokes.text: I don't know if I could be impartial, Mr. Condon, given that the defendant is a Negro.
jokes.setup: Larry is asked if he can be impartial on jury
jokes.punchline: His shocking use of outdated racial terminology to get dismissed
jokes.explanation: Shockingly inappropriate but perfectly Larry - willing to say anything to avoid responsibility

jokes.text: I'm not looking for a sound system, my friend.
jokes.setup: Drug dealer offers 'hydroponic scientific stuff'
jokes.punchline: Larry's confusion about 'hydroponic' making him think it's electronics
jokes.explanation: Larry's square misunderstanding of drug terminology

jokes.text: Look, you can pay me $200 bucks or go fuck yourself. I don't need to sell the drugs, the drugs'll sell themselves.
jokes.setup: Larry questions the drug price
jokes.punchline: Dealer's aggressive business philosophy about drug sales
jokes.explanation: Harsh reality of drug dealing vs Larry's negotiation expectations

jokes.text: I'm not gonna use the carpool lane by myself. 'Cause l... I don't want to.
jokes.setup: Jeff suggests using carpool lane to get to game faster
jokes.punchline: Larry's weak moral stance against carpool violation
jokes.explanation: Sets up Larry's moral flexibility - he'll follow rules until he needs to break them

jokes.text: I'm not gonna use the carpool lane by myself. 'Cause l... I don't want to.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why he won't cheat carpool lane
jokes.punchline: Weak, circular reasoning for his moral stance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's arbitrary moral boundaries

jokes.text: Hey, Daddy. You wanna date with Mama?
jokes.setup: Larry stops near a prostitute
jokes.punchline: Prostitute's direct sales pitch in baby talk
jokes.explanation: Fairly standard prostitute dialogue, sets up the situation

jokes.text: See, actually, I'm not really interested in any sex, per se. Per... you one of them freaky motherfuckers or something?
jokes.setup: Larry tries to explain he doesn't want sex
jokes.punchline: Monena's assumption that he must be into weird stuff
jokes.explanation: Clash between Larry's awkward explanation and street reality

jokes.text: Exactly, I can give four blowjobs an hour. Four blowjobs an hour? Yes, I'm good.
jokes.setup: Monena explains her hourly rate calculation
jokes.punchline: The businesslike discussion of sexual efficiency metrics
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable business discussion about sexual services

jokes.text: Honey, I got a red snapper that talks to you. You know what it's saying? I'm charging way too much.
jokes.setup: Monena brags about her anatomy
jokes.punchline: The talking fish metaphor admitting she overcharges
jokes.explanation: Creative sexual metaphor with unexpected self-deprecating twist

jokes.text: Are you familiar with Henry Clay? He was the Great Compromiser. A good compromise is when both parties are dissatisfied.
jokes.setup: Larry negotiates with prostitute over price
jokes.punchline: Invoking American political history during prostitute negotiation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - Larry applies political philosophy to streetwalker negotiations

jokes.text: That's two tickets. I'm meeting a friend there actually. No, I'm gonna go to the game or you're gonna take me back to the corner or I'm gonna call my motherfucking pimp.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to explain why he has two tickets
jokes.punchline: Monena's aggressive threat involving her pimp
jokes.explanation: Escalation of Larry's predicament as Monena takes control

jokes.text: Ow, baseball! I ain't never been to no baseball game. This gonna be cute.
jokes.setup: Monena realizes she's going to baseball game
jokes.punchline: Her unexpected excitement about new experience
jokes.explanation: Humanizing moment showing Monena's genuine excitement

jokes.text: I know the seats ain't way the fuck up here. You got a problem with the seats? You said we had good seats, club seats and shit.
jokes.setup: They arrive at their seats in upper deck
jokes.punchline: Monena's justified anger at Larry's overselling of seat quality
jokes.explanation: Monena calling out Larry's exaggeration about seat quality

jokes.text: I will pull a titty out in this thing. Don't... I will pull a titty out... Don't you dare do that.
jokes.setup: Larry seems embarrassed to be seen with Monena
jokes.punchline: Monena's threat to expose herself as punishment
jokes.explanation: Escalating threat that terrifies Larry with public embarrassment

jokes.text: Your father's dead. It's a memorial. We used to go to games together. It makes me feel close to him.
jokes.setup: Larry asks to sit in the empty seat next to Marty
jokes.punchline: Marty's emotional explanation that it's a memorial to his dead father
jokes.explanation: Larry's callous pragmatism crashes into genuine grief and sentiment

jokes.text: If you're ever looking for a good blowjob at a reasonable rate... she's your gal.
jokes.setup: Larry gives Monena a recommendation
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact testimonial for prostitution services
jokes.explanation: Absurd normalization of prostitution as business recommendation

jokes.text: Hey, Leo, why don't you give him a push?
jokes.setup: Marty's car won't start and he needs help
jokes.punchline: Larry suggesting the dead father should help push the car
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark callback to dead father, showing Larry's complete social tone-deafness

jokes.text: I didn't pick her up for sex. I picked her up so I could use the diamond lane.
jokes.setup: Larry explains his situation with the prostitute
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous practicality of hiring a prostitute just for carpool access
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David logic - subverting prostitution for traffic convenience

jokes.text: Forget everything you've ever thought about golf.
jokes.setup: Larry finally gets the golf tip in exchange for the ride
jokes.punchline: The anticlimactic and vague nature of the supposedly valuable tip
jokes.explanation: Payoff of running gag with disappointing but fitting vague advice

jokes.text: It's not my marijuana! I was just holding the jacket! This isn't even my jacket! It doesn't even fit me!
jokes.setup: Larry gets arrested for marijuana possession at airport
jokes.punchline: His increasingly desperate and contradictory denials
jokes.explanation: Escalating panic as all Larry's schemes converge disastrously

jokes.text: You don't need no damn marijuana. I got the best shit in town. Chronic.
jokes.setup: Larry explains buying marijuana for his father
jokes.punchline: Monena revealing she has better drugs all along
jokes.explanation: Ironic revelation that Larry could have avoided the whole drug dealer encounter

jokes.text: What the fuck is schwag? You don't even know what you bought.
jokes.setup: Larry mentions he bought 'schwag'
jokes.punchline: Monena mocking Larry's ignorance about drug quality
jokes.explanation: Highlights Larry's naive venture into drug dealing

jokes.text: Tomorrow? This ain't motherfucking pussy on layaway!
jokes.setup: Larry asks to pay Monena tomorrow instead of today
jokes.punchline: Monena's crude retail metaphor about sexual services
jokes.explanation: Brilliant crude metaphor combining retail and sex work terminology

jokes.text: You trying to be hip all and shit.
jokes.setup: Larry says 'cool de la' trying to sound hip
jokes.punchline: Monena calling out his pathetic attempt at coolness
jokes.explanation: Monena seeing through Larry's desperate attempt to connect

jokes.text: Yeah yeah, which you proceeded to blow.
jokes.setup: Monena complains about only getting one hot dog
jokes.punchline: Larry's double entendre about 'blowing' the hot dog
jokes.explanation: Obvious sexual pun that feels forced rather than clever

jokes.text: Look what we got for you. Oh, that's a joint. Yeah, this is a joint. Very good, Daddy.
jokes.setup: Monena presents the marijuana to Nat
jokes.punchline: Nat's unexpectedly casual recognition and Monena's praise
jokes.explanation: Unexpected generational knowledge gap subversion

jokes.text: It's a reefer. / Yeah, absolutely. You light it up. You go like this. Put it in your mouth... You gotta inhale. Hold it in your lungs.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining marijuana to his elderly father
jokes.punchline: Detailed instructions like teaching someone to use new technology
jokes.explanation: Good generational comedy with Larry as patient instructor for drug use

jokes.text: Dis chronic is the shizmik. For shizzle my nizzle.
jokes.setup: Monena teaches Nat to speak Ebonics while high
jokes.punchline: Elderly Jewish man attempting hip-hop slang pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Absurd cultural collision between elderly Jewish man and street slang

jokes.text: Gei kucken ahfen yam.
jokes.setup: Nat is trying to speak Ebonics but reverts to Yiddish while high
jokes.punchline: Pure Yiddish phrase while attempting to be cool
jokes.explanation: Cultural identity assertion through native language while high

jokes.text: You're not gonna do a fuckin' thing! TV TV TV, that's all you like to do! Read a fucking book!
jokes.setup: Nat is high and watching TV
jokes.punchline: His marijuana-fueled rant about his own TV watching habits
jokes.explanation: Marijuana-induced self-criticism and rage at his own habits

jokes.text: You know something? This stuff? I can see. I swear to you, I'm not lying to you. You're coming through so crystal clear. It's a miracle.
jokes.setup: Nat describing improved vision from marijuana
jokes.punchline: Claiming perfect clarity while obviously very high
jokes.explanation: Good ironic comedy - claiming improved vision while clearly impaired, sweet character moment

jokes.text: Oh my God. It's a hooker!
jokes.setup: Nat finally realizes who Monena is after getting high with her
jokes.punchline: His delayed recognition after bonding with her
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing for realization after he's already bonded with her

jokes.text: Mr. Owe-Monena-$200. You said five hours and it was seven, honey.
jokes.setup: Larry encounters Monena at the courthouse
jokes.punchline: Her business-like accounting of overtime hours owed
jokes.explanation: Monena's professional approach to collecting her debt in formal setting

jokes.text: Well, Mr. David, what do you have to say for yourself? Well, you see, Your Honor, my father has this condition.
jokes.setup: The racist judge from jury duty is now presiding over Larry's case
jokes.punchline: Larry about to explain the marijuana to the same judge he offended
jokes.explanation: Perfect narrative circle bringing Larry's racist comment back to haunt him

tmdb_overview: To get to a Dodgers game, Larry finds a creative way to use the HOV lane.  Then, he finds himself in a bit of trouble when he gets his father medicinal marijuana.
